preamble: The shift from a mid-back vowel [o] to [ou] is the back-vowel counterpart to [e] > [ei], often occurring in similar phonological contexts.

examples.note: Modern English development where historical long [o:] diphthongized.
Similar diphthongization of mid-back long vowels in Standard Dutch.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
